mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) signals strong labor market conditions, which correlate with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in HOUST (housing starts) below 1,250 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST decline periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in INDPRO (industrial production) and declining RSAFS (consumer credit) indicates a supply-side expansion constrained by weak consumer demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-moving INDPRO/RSAFS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in FEDFUNDS above 2.5% during a CPIAUCSL inflationary trend signals monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL tightening periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDPC1 (GDP) above 3% indicates robust economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in UNRATE (unemployment rate) below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE decline periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in RSAFS (consumer credit) and falling INDPRO (industrial production) indicates weak demand despite credit expansion, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in RSAFS/INDPRO divergent periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS below 2.0% during rising CPIAUCSL (inflation) signals accommodative policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL easing periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
